CHEQUP
  • initial_condition
  • ionization_routines module
  • View page source

ionization_routines module

ionization_routines.get_adk_parameters()[source]

Return arrays needed for calculation of ADK ionization rates

ionization_routines.get_fraction_and_temperature_multispecies(a0, tau, lambd, ell, adk_prefactors, adk_powers, adk_exp_prefactors, source_indices, target_indices, charges, initial_populations, npts_per_wavelength=80)[source]

a0: Peak laser amplitude tau: laser FWHM duration lambd: laser wavelength ell: polarization vector n0: electron density if the plasma was fully ionized charges : Charge state of each species (e.g., [0, 1, 0, 1, 2, 3] for H0, H1, N0, N1, N2, N3) initial_populations : Initial population fractions for each species (should sum to 1.0)

ionization_routines.load_intensity_profile(filename)[source]

Load intensity profile from txt or csv file

Parameters:

filename : str, Path to file containing intensity data

Returns:

intensity_array : 1D or 2D array intensity values (W/m^2)

ionization_routines.process_intensity_array_multispecies(intensity_nd, lambd, tau, ell, adk_prefactors, adk_powers, adk_exp_prefactors, source_indices, target_indices, charges, species_keys, initial_populations, output_file=None, grid_extent=None, n_total=1e+24)[source]

Process nD intensity array for multi-species plasma

Parameters:

intensity_ndnD array

Laser intensity profile I in W/m^2

lambdfloat

Laser wavelength (m)

taufloat

Laser pulse FWHM duration (s)

ellarray-like

Polarization vector [2-element array]

initial_populationsdictionary

Dictionary with elements of species_keys as keys and initial population fractions as values If an element from species_keys is not in the dictionary, it is assumed to be 0

output_filestr, optional

Filename to save openPMD output with radius, temperature and densities

grid_extentdict, optional

Grid extent for openPMD output

n_totalfloat

Total number density in m^-3 for converting fractions to densities (default: 1e24)

Returns:

all_populationsnD array

Population fractions for all species (shape: [*intensity_nd.shape, len(species_keys)])

T_arraynD array

Electron temperatures in eV, same (shape: intensity_nd)

ionization_routines.save_to_openpmd(grid_extent, all_populations, Te_eV, output_file, species_keys, xmom=0, ymom=0, zmom=0, Th_eV=None)[source]

Save with all species densities (m^-3) to an openPMD file
